strategy: agentic chunking via Gemini (headline + summary + original text)

pages.text:
Calipers measure how thick your skin is. The more fat you have under your skin, the
thicker it is. By pinching together your skin, you create a skinfold. The caliper measures
the thickness of that skinfold. This allows us to objectively monitor your fat loss.

When using calipers, the most important thing by far is to always measure the exact
same sites. Try to find birthmarks, moles, creases, muscle protrusions or other markers
that can identify exactly where you pinch your skin together. Consistency in your
measurement technique is everything. There are no magic locations, so think of the
locations below as a rough area nearby which you have to find a spot you can easily
locate again.

Second, always pinch the same amount of skin: as much skin as possible generally
works well if you don't have much fat on a location. Otherwise, be sure to have a
consistent method to grab a certain amount of skin. You generally want to start each
measurement with your thumb and index finger a consistent distance away from each
other, normally about one inch.

Third, to improve reliability, measure each skinfold site 5 times and note down all 5
measurements in your log so we can calculate the average (or mode). Take
measurements at least once a week.

Caliper brands
Almost as important as your measurement technique is having good calipers. Most
calipers are poor. Depending on your budget and confidence in your proficiency with the
calipers, the following calipers are recommended. You should be able to find them on
Amazon, eBay or with a Google search. The official distributor websites for calipers
often don't sell the calipers in retail.

King of calipers: Lange calipers and Harpenden calipers are the 2 giants that have
long served as the industry benchmark of gold standard quality. They are the standard
by which other calipers are measured, even in scientific research. However, they come
with a hefty price tag. You'll be lucky to find one under $200. Harpenden calipers are
generally closer to $300 even.

Best buy: At $20, the Fat-O-Meter has been found in research to give practically the
same results as Lange calipers for a fraction of the cost. For $50, The Body Caliper has
been validated against both the Harpenden calipers and body fat percentage
measurement via underwater weighing. Unfortunately, official distribution for both has
stopped, so your only chance to find them is often on Amazon or eBay. Make sure you
get an original model, as there are some fakes/replicas going around.

If you can't find either, Slimguide calipers are your best bet.

pages.text:
Suprailiac
Grab a skinfold above and parallel to your right iliac crest, the ridge of your right hip.
The skinfold will be roughly diagonal.

Umbilical/abdominal
Grab a vertical skinfold about an inch to the right of your belly button.

Thigh
While standing on your left leg, grab a vertical skinfold in the middle of your right thigh. If
you cannot find any spots to use as reference points, use the length of your hand as a
reference for how far up the leg you take the skinfold. It's often hard to grab enough skin
on the thigh, so this measurement may hurt a bit.

Calf
While seated with your right lower leg relaxed in a vertical position and 90° knee and
ankle angles, grab a vertical skinfold on the inside of your right calf where it is thickest.
Example.

full_text:
--- Page 1 ---
HOW TO USE CALIPERS


--- Page 2 ---
Calipers measure how thick your skin is. The more fat you have under your skin, the
thicker it is. By pinching together your skin, you create a skinfold. The caliper measures
the thickness of that skinfold. This allows us to objectively monitor your fat loss.

When using calipers, the most important thing by far is to always measure the exact

measurement technique is everything. There are no magic locations, so think of the
locations below as a rough area nearby which you have to find a spot you can easily
locate again.

Second, always pinch the same amount of skin: as much skin as possible generally
works well if you don't have much fat on a location. Otherwise, be sure to have a
consistent method to grab a certain amount of skin. You generally want to start each
measurement with your thumb and index finger a consistent distance away from each
other, normally about one inch.

Third, to improve reliability, measure each skinfold site 5 times and note down all 5
measurements in your log so we can calculate the average (or mode). Take
measurements at least once a week.


--- Page 3 ---

--- Page 4 ---

The skinfold will be roughly diagonal.

--- Page 5 ---

Example.
